description: Fix bugs in plugin-tools and shared packages discovered during backend audit: watch mode double-rebuild, non-string config elements crash, invalid IPv4 in URL patterns, and percentage-based rgb() in icon validation.

userStories.title: Fix watch mode double-rebuild due to lingering debounce timer
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want watch mode to not trigger redundant double-rebuilds when file changes arrive during an active build.
acceptanceCriteria: When rebuild() starts, the debounce timer (debounceTimer) is cleared before checking pendingRebuild
A file change during an active build results in exactly one follow-up rebuild (via pendingRebuild), not two
Normal debounce behavior for changes outside of an active build is preserved

userStories.title: Guard against non-string elements in config.localPlugins during build
userStories.description: As a plugin developer with a manually-edited config, I want the build command to gracefully handle non-string elements in localPlugins instead of crashing with an unhelpful TypeError.
acceptanceCriteria: config.localPlugins is filtered to only include string elements before use in registerInConfig
Non-string elements (null, numbers, objects) are silently skipped
The build succeeds when config.localPlugins contains mixed types
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts around lines 206-207.

Current code in registerInConfig():
```typescript
const localPlugins: string[] = Array.isArray(config.localPlugins) ? config.localPlugins : [];
```

Fix: Add a filter:
```typescript
const localPlugins: string[] = Array.isArray(config.localPlugins)
  ? (config.localPlugins as unknown[]).filter((p): p is string => typeof p === 'string')
  : [];
```

This matches the defensive pattern used in the MCP server's config.ts parseConfigRecord function (line 180-181).

userStories.title: Validate IPv4 octets in URL pattern validation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want validateUrlPattern to reject invalid IPv4 addresses like 999.999.999.999 so that I get a build-time error instead of a silently non-functional plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: validateUrlPattern rejects URL patterns with IPv4 addresses containing octets > 255
validateUrlPattern accepts valid IPv4 addresses like 192.168.1.1, 127.0.0.1, 10.0.0.1
Error message clearly states which octet is out of range
Tests cover: valid IPs, edge cases (0.0.0.0, 255.255.255.255), invalid IPs (256.0.0.1, 999.999.999.999)

userStories.title: Handle percentage-based rgb() in SVG icon color validation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the icon validator to correctly parse percentage-based rgb() colors so that saturated colors in inactive icons are properly detected and converted to grayscale.
acceptanceCriteria: parseColor correctly parses rgb(100%, 50%, 0%) as {r: 255, g: 128, b: 0}
parseColor correctly parses rgba(100%, 0%, 0%, 0.5) as {r: 255, g: 0, b: 0, a: 0.5}
isAchromaticColor returns false for rgb(100%, 0%, 0%) (it's saturated red, not gray)
convertColorToGray converts percentage-based rgb colors to grayscale
generateInactiveIcon converts percentage-based rgb() colors to grayscale
Integer-based rgb() continues to work as before
Tests cover percentage-based rgb() in both comma-separated and modern space-separated syntax
userStories.notes:
File: platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.ts.

The parseColor function (around line 220) has regexes for various color formats. The legacy comma-separated rgb() regex at line 228:
```typescript
const legacyRgbMatch = value.match(/^rgba?\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)/);
```
only matches integer values. Extend it to also match percentage values:
```typescript
const legacyRgbMatch = value.match(/^rgba?\(\s*(\d+%?)\s*,\s*(\d+%?)\s*,\s*(\d+%?)/);
```
Then when extracting values, check for the % suffix and convert: if value ends with '%', parse as Number(value.slice(0, -1)) * 255 / 100, clamped to 0-255.

Also check convertColorToGray (around line 450) for the same issue — it has its own regex matching that may also need the percentage extension.

Similarly check the modern space-separated regex patterns in the same function.

Test file: platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.test.ts.
